sections.heading: Market Overview
sections.content: GCC markets opened mixed today. Saudi Tadawul index edged up 0.3% on energy gains, while Dubai Financial Market slipped 0.1% amid profit-taking. Qatar Exchange remained flat. Oil prices held above $85/bbl supporting regional sentiment.

items.title: Saudi Arabia Announces New Tourism Visa Waivers
items.summary: The Kingdom expanded visa-free entry to 7 additional countries, aiming to boost visitor numbers by 15% in 2025.

items.title: UAE Non-Oil Business Activity Accelerates
items.summary: S&P Global PMI rose to 55.2 in March, driven by strong demand and new export orders.

items.title: QatarEnergy Signs Long-Term LNG Deal with India
items.summary: A 20-year agreement to supply 1.5 million tonnes per annum strengthens energy ties.

sections.heading: What to Watch Today
sections.content: Saudi industrial production data for February due at 10:00 AM Riyadh time. UAE Central Bank monetary policy meeting expected to hold rates.
